title: Agent from Beginner to Mastery
beat: Open by reframing the topic: this is not a deck about another chatbot.
script: Set the frame: Hermes is valuable when language turns into inspected work. We are moving from prompt novelty to an execution system that can read, act, check, and write back.

title: Not a chat box. An execution system.
beat: Separate the model from the system around it.
script: The model is one layer. The useful system adds tools, skills, context, and QA. The test is simple: does the work end as advice, or does it end as a checked deliverable?

title: Start with one real task.
beat: Keep the first Hermes win small enough to verify.
script: Do not start by automating everything. Start with Hermes running, a provider configured, Doctor passing, and one artifact delivered. That is the first reliable loop.

title: Tools decide the execution boundary.
beat: Tool access is both capability and permission design.
script: Without tools, an agent can suggest. With Hermes tools, it can read files, run commands, verify output, and write results back. The boundary has to be visible.

title: Package experience into reusable workflows.
beat: A Hermes skill is an operating procedure, not a prompt folder.
script: A useful skill says when Hermes should load it, what the user must provide, what SKILL.md defines, what requires confirmation, and how the output is judged.

title: Hermes needs context it can act on.
beat: Context is for stable decisions, not memory hoarding.
script: Hermes becomes more reliable when it can see project memory, current task state, and user working preferences. The goal is fewer repeated explanations and better decisions.

title: Complex work needs separated responsibility.
beat: Agent teams work only when handoffs are accountable.
script: The value is not parallelism for show. Design, build, QA, and deploy agents each own a bounded output and a visible check.

title: Make agents part of the desk.
beat: Tie the operating pattern together before the material demos.
script: Mastery means the workflow compounds: small loops, tool access, reusable skills, project context, and visible QA. That is how an agent becomes part of daily work.

title: Generated atmosphere can replace generic placeholders.
beat: Show where image generation belongs in this workflow.
script: For exact labels, keep text in HTML or SVG. Use GPT Image 2 where atmosphere matters: cover texture, mood, workbench context, and non-textual visual evidence.

title: Motion should explain the loop, not replace the slide.
beat: The video is a material producer, not a separate deck format.
script: This page embeds a real Remotion MP4. The clip explains the read, act, check, write-back loop. Presenter and deploy can later decide whether to keep video or use a poster fallback.

title: Hermes mastery is a working system.
beat: End with the repeatable operating path.
script: Close on the state transfer: run one real Hermes task, package the repeated win into a skill, keep context and QA visible, then split complex work into accountable handoffs.
